verified.name: FastDOL
verified.tagline: FastDOL — free tier with API key
verified.description: FastDOL exposes a free-tier JSON API. Register for an API key if required; we verified a public or demo endpoint.

info.title: FastDOL API
info.description:
FastDOL aggregates employer compliance and enforcement data from every major
US federal source — OSHA, WHD, MSHA, EPA ECHO, NLRB, FMCSA, OFLC, SAM.gov,
CMS, CPSC, NHTSA, SEC, USAspending, BLS — plus the UVA Corporate Prosecution
Registry, into one REST API keyed by a stable `employer_id`.

**Authentication.** Every request takes an `X-Api-Key` header. Click
**Authorize** and paste your key to try endpoints live below.

**Base URL.** `https://api.fastdol.com` — all paths are under `/v1`.

**Bulk access.** Filtered slices via the async **Bulk export** endpoint
(≤100k rows); the entire dataset as weekly point-in-time Parquet via the
**Snapshots** endpoints.

get.description:
Search employers by name, EIN, zip, state, or NAICS.

Returns a flat list of employer locations sorted by risk score (worst first).
Each result includes `related_locations_count` showing how many locations
share the same normalized employer name — useful for national chains.

Parent company risk rollup — aggregate risk across all locations.

Returns company-wide statistics (total inspections, violations, penalties,
risk distribution) plus a paginated list of individual locations sorted
by risk score (worst first).

Premium feature for enterprise customers evaluating national employers.

Lookup employers by FEIN (Federal Employer Identification Number).

Returns all locations matching the given EIN. Insurance professionals
identify employers by FEIN — this is the primary lookup for underwriting
integrations.

EIN format: 9 digits (e.g., '943067788' or '94-3067788').

Get inspection history with violation detail for an employer.

Metered: one lookup per call. Previously unmetered, which made it trivial
for a free-tier key to scrape the entire product by combining the sitemap
endpoint (employer IDs) with this one (full inspection history per id).

Per-event OSHA Severe Injury Reports (29 CFR 1904.39) for an employer.

Each row is an injury the employer self-reported within 24 hours
-- in-patient hospitalization, amputation, or loss of an eye.
Distinct from /employers/{id}/inspections (inspection-triggered):
SIR is injury-triggered, so the same event may or may not have a
related inspection (see inspection_activity_nr -- often NULL).

Metered: one lookup per call, same as inspections / SEC enforcement.
Newest event first.

Per-case WHD (Wage & Hour Division) enforcement records for an
employer.

Each row is one closed case carrying backwages assessed
(`bw_amt`) and the count of underpaid employees
(`ee_violtd_cnt`). Covers FLSA, FMLA, MSPA, H-1B/H-2A/H-2B, CCPA,
and any other DOL WHD-administered statute.

Metered: one lookup per call, same pattern as the other detail
endpoints. Most-recent case first.

Per-citation CMS nursing-home deficiency list for a facility.

Each row is one Health Deficiency citation from CMS Care Compare --
survey date, deficiency tag (F-tag), the textual description,
scope-severity letter (A-L), whether corrected, and the inspection
cycle (standard survey vs complaint vs infection control).

Lookup: resolves cms_ccn from employer_profile. Only facilities with
a populated CMS Certification Number have deficiency rosters. Other
employers return 200 with empty results.

Severity coloring at the UI layer follows CMS conventions:
  A-C: isolated potential harm (mild)
  D-F: pattern potential harm (moderate)
  G-I: actual harm (high)
  J-L: immediate jeopardy (critical, burnt-umber)

Ordered most-recent-survey first, then severity within survey.

employer_id.title: Employer Id
employer_id.description: FastDOL employer_id (UUID) for an exact match

lookups.title: Lookups
lookups.description: 1–100 lookup items per synchronous request (use CSV upload for more).

file.title: File
file.description: CSV file with employer names and optional location fields

parent_only.title: Parent Only
parent_only.description: Roll up to one row per parent company

ExportFilters.title: ExportFilters
ExportFilters.description:
Filter surface for bulk exports — richer than the interactive
search endpoint because enterprise analysts need structured queries
(e.g. "top 50K employers by fatality rate with OSHA penalties >
$100K in construction NAICS").

filters.description: At least one filter is required

sort_by.title: Sort By
sort_by.description: One of: employer_id, fatalities, inspections, penalties, violations, whd_backwages, whd_cases

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-Api-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Enterprise API key (issued by FastDOL, prefix `emp_live_`). Sent on every request.

tags.name: Employers
tags.description: Search, look up, and pull the full multi-agency compliance profile and per-source detail for any US employer establishment.

tags.name: Industry benchmarks
tags.description: BLS SOII industry-average injury rates (TRIR/DART) by 4-digit NAICS, for peer comparison.

/api/v1/translate.description: EN↔CN bilingual translation with cultural adaptation

/api/v1/compliance-check.description: Full content compliance review: Chinese advertising law + platform rules + short-video content safety. Supports script/hook/caption/voiceover/title types.

/api/v1/compliance-batch.description: Batch review up to 20 items at once

/api/v1/compliance-report.description: Generate formal compliance report (audit trail, defensible in enforcement)

/api/v1/seo-optimize.description: SEO title, description, and keyword optimization

sampleJson.description: SpaceTraders is a headless API and fleet-management game where players can work together or against each other to trade, explore, expand, and conquer in a dynamic and growing universe. Build your own UI, write automated scripts, or just play the game from the comfort of your terminal. The game is currently in alpha and is under active development.
